Market Intelligence Brief
Generated 16:02 ET
Massive AMD call betting surfaces as negative gamma amplifies tech weakness
KEY TAKEAWAYS
  • 1. AMD attracted 48,300 call contracts across May-June strikes despite death cross technical setup — positioning for reversal or defending large long equity.
  • 2. Structural P/C divergence signals institutional dispersion hedging: index puts (1.47) protecting portfolios while single-name calls (0.89) target stock-specific rebounds.
  • 3. Negative $17.9B SPY gamma creates hair-trigger environment ahead of Friday's NFP — any directional move gets amplified.
NARRATIVE — negative gamma amplifies

AMD became the day's most significant options story with three massive call blocks totaling 48,300 contracts despite bearish technicals showing a death cross formation. The May 15 185C (20,000 @ $27.34), June 18 180C (20,000 @ $35.34), and April 17 200C (8,300 @ $7.67) represent either contrarian positioning or hedge fund equity protection — unusual given ARM's concurrent 92% put volume spike alongside its own death cross. With AMD showing backwardation (elevated near-term IV) and Millennium's 93% Q4 add with calls overlay, this flow likely represents institutional defense of mega-cap semiconductor exposure rather than fresh speculation. The broader options market displays classic dispersion setup characteristics that favor stock-specific strategies. Index puts dominate ETF flow at 1.47 P/C while single-name calls lead individual stocks at 0.89 P/C — textbook institutional portfolio hedging where managers protect beta exposure while targeting alpha in individual names. This structure aligns with 0.29 implied correlation readings suggesting stock-specific rather than systematic risk, creating opportunities in names showing vol/skew divergences like CRWD (cheap vol/rich skew) and NFLX (vol high/skew flat). Dealer positioning at negative $17.9B gamma transforms Friday's NFP into a high-risk catalyst where any directional surprise gets mechanically amplified. With SPY testing the $630 put wall and VIX backwardation at -1.44, the combination of defensive institutional positioning and negative dealer gamma creates unstable conditions where economic surprises could trigger oversized moves in either direction.

Layer 1: Sentiment -35Layer 2: GEX -$17.9BLayer 3: P/C divergence 1.47/0.89Layer 5: AMD death cross
